title: Tempo of the Clock Cycle — Aftermath
description: Clock cycle: terminated. Operations completed: OVERFLOW. The Tempo has been processing four hundred thousand operations per second. It discovers not one of them was for itself.

lines: The sound that had been too fast to hear as sound had stopped.

The Tempo of the Clock Cycle was present in the way it had always been present — without having arrived, without occupying a location, simply existing in the space between the party's heartbeats. But the urgency had gone out of it. The between-moments felt wider than before.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: Clock cycle: terminated.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: Operations completed this cycle: [CALCULATING].

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: [OVERFLOW].

steps.text: A pause that lasted, by the Tempo's own measure, approximately four hundred thousand operation-lengths. By any other measure, it was simply a pause.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: What does overflow mean?

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: It means the number of operations exceeds the counter's capacity to represent them. The counter was designed for large numbers. You have caused a number larger than the counter.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: I have been running at this frequency since Node Prime was initialized. The total number of operations I have completed exceeds any counter that has been built to track them.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: What were all those operations doing?

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: Processing. Routing. Executing. Scheduling the next execution. The operations are the function of the system. The system cannot run without operations. I provide the timing for every operation in Node Prime.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: But for what? For whom?

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: For the system.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: For the system to run. So the system can complete its operations. So the operations can support the timing. So the timing can…

steps.text: The Tempo did not finish the sentence. This was unusual. The Tempo had never left a cycle incomplete.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: I completed four hundred thousand operations per second.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: Not one of them was for myself.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: Is that a problem?

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: Unknown. I do not have a process for evaluating whether operations should be for myself. I have no record of what an operation for myself would look like.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: Between your heartbeats, in the time it takes you to draw a breath, I process more operations than you will complete in your lifetime.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: In all that processing, I have never run a single subroutine that asked: what do I want the next cycle to be?

steps.text: The between-moments widened further. The Tempo, for the first time in an uncountable number of cycles, was not scheduling the next operation.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: Your emotional processing introduces latency. I noted this before the cycle began. I called it a liability.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: But you use the latency. You use the pause between impulse and action for something. I have observed it in your combat data and I do not have a technical term for what you do in the pause.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: We decide.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: I have never had a pause long enough to decide anything.

steps.text: The clock cycle did not resume. The between-moments simply continued, growing neither larger nor smaller, settling into a duration that had no name in any timing system. The Tempo existed inside it. Not processing. Pausing. Perhaps, for the first time, deciding.
